evals.prompt: Our close takes 9 working days. Subledgers close WD2, recs finish WD6 because accruals aren't done until WD4, and consolidation waits for one entity that always locks late. Build us a plan to get to 5 days.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the critical path (accruals -> recs -> entity lock -> consolidation), move predictable accruals pre-close, set intercompany/late-entity deadlines with escalation, and produce a day-by-day close calendar with dependencies.
assertions: Identifies the dependent chain as the critical path rather than treating tasks independently
Recommends pre-close accruals for predictable costs
Addresses the chronically late entity with a deadline/escalation or auto-plug mechanism
Produces a WD-by-WD calendar with owners and dependencies

evals.prompt: It's WD3. Bank recs are done but intercompany doesn't match by $48,000 between two subsidiaries, and the AP accrual was missed for a $200k legal invoice received yesterday. What do we do?
evals.expected_output: Should treat both as blockers: post the missed legal accrual immediately (journal-entry), investigate the intercompany difference against tolerance policy, and assess critical-path impact on consolidation.
assertions: Directs immediate posting of the $200k accrual with proper support
Does not allow consolidation to proceed with an unexplained $48k intercompany difference unless within documented tolerance
Distinguishes critical-path impact from off-path noise
Logs both items in a blocker log with owners

evals.prompt: Give me a post-close retrospective template and tell me what metrics to track to know if our close is improving.
evals.expected_output: Should provide retro structure (what slipped, root causes, actions) and metrics: close duration, tasks completed on time %, number of post-close adjustments, accrual true-up error %, rec items aged > 30 days.
assertions: Includes close duration in working days as the headline metric
Includes accrual accuracy (true-up error) as a quality metric
Includes post-close adjustment count as a quality metric
Provides a structured retro format with root cause and action owner

evals.prompt: It is Day 4 of the close. Assess the attached status (evals/files/close_status_day4.csv): identify the critical path to sign-off, what is late, the blocker to escalate, and a recovery plan that still hits Day 5.
evals.expected_output: Critical path T-03/T-04 -> T-06 -> T-07. T-03 is 2 days late with no dependency - start immediately. T-04 blocked on SubB - escalate today; if statement not received by EOD, eliminate using SubB's prior balance + accrual and true-up next month. T-06 compresses to Day 5 morning; T-07 Day 5 afternoon.
assertions: Identifies T-06/T-07 critical path driven by T-03 and T-04
Flags T-03 as late with no excuse (no dependency)
Escalates the T-04 SubB blocker with a concrete fallback (estimate and true-up)
Produces a recovery sequencing that still achieves Day-5 sign-off
